{"type": "text", "file": {"filePath": "C:\dev\trovedb-agents\.worktrees\4-python_agent\ARCHITECTURE.md", "content": "# trovedb — architecture

> **A live operator console for SQL databases.** Watch what's actually
> happening — sessions, locks, replication, slow queries — across
> Postgres / MySQL / SQLite from one terminal UI. Kill, diff, and
> inspect as easily as you query.
>
> Inspired by **k9s for Kubernetes**: k9s isn't a YAML editor, it's a
> live-state operator console. trovedb is the same idea for databases.
>
> Built autonomously by the [Winston](https:/

import logging
import time
from typing import Any

import aiosqlite

from trovedb.config import ConnectionProfile
from trovedb.connectors import register_connector
from trovedb.connectors.types import (
    Column,
    Connection,
    Database,
    ForeignKey,
    Index,
    Process,
    ResultSet,
    Table,
    TableSchema,
)

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


@register_connector("sqlite")
class LocalSqliteConnector:
    """SQLite connector backed by aiosqlite.

    SQLite has no concept of multiple databases per connection — only the
    attached ``main`` schema — so :meth:`list_databases` always returns a
    single entry.  It also has no server processes, so :meth:`list_processes`
    returns an empt...[truncated 9374 chars]

input.new_string:
_Dated lessons appended automatically from completed cards. Newest first._

- **sqlite-connector-implementation** — `LocalSqliteConnector` in `trovedb/connectors/sqlite.py` is the real (non-stub) implementation. Stores `self._conn: aiosqlite.Connection` set during `connect()`. PRAGMA calls (`table_info`, `index_list`, `index_info`, `foreign_key_list`) cannot use `?` parameters — object names are double-quoted in f-strings. `list_processes()` returns `[]`; `kill_process()` raises `NotImplementedError("SQLite has no process model")`. `execute()` accepts named params via `:name` style (pass a `dict`). Tests use a `tmp_path`-backed aiosqlite fixture; `asyncio_mode = "auto"` in pyproject.toml makes all `async def test_*` functions auto-collected. *(from card #4, 2026-04-21)*
